candidates.quote: At best Jonos Bracken had five hundred men about the castle.
candidates.note: Besieging/Iron Throne side; Jonos commands the force starving out Raventree.

candidates.quote: Tytos Blackwood has not bent the knee,
candidates.note: Defending side; Tytos holds Raventree against Jonos's siege.

candidates.quote: I mean to deal with Lord Tytos myself. It won’t require a siege tower.
candidates.note: Jaime personally resolves the siege via parley rather than assault.

candidates.quote: Lord Jaime, you must go to Raventree. So long as it is Jonos at his gates Tytos will never yield, but I know he will bend his knee for you.
candidates.note: Karyl advocates for Jaime's intervention; organizational/advisory, not a combatant here.

candidates.quote: Daven proposed that they drag it all to Raventree and use it there.
candidates.note: Daven's logistical advice about siege equipment (Jaime overrules it).

candidates.quote: At the Bridge of Skulls he had met the Weeper and three hundred wildlings and won a bloody battle.
candidates.note: Night's Watch side commander.

candidates.quote: At the Bridge of Skulls he had met the Weeper and three hundred wildlings and won a bloody battle.
candidates.note: Wildling/free folk raiding-party side commander.

candidates.quote: More than a hundred brothers slain, among them Ser Endrew Tarth and Ser Aladale Wynch.
candidates.note: Killed in the battle.

candidates.quote: More than a hundred brothers slain, among them Ser Endrew Tarth and Ser Aladale Wynch.
candidates.note: Killed in the battle.

candidates.quote: a raven had come from the west, from Ser Denys Mallister.
candidates.note: Administrative/logistical role relaying word of the battle to Castle Black; not shown personally fighting at the bridge itself.

candidates.quote: Anguy’s arrow took him in the chest.
candidates.note: Opens the battle by killing the rooftop sentinel; overlaps with sub-beat node anguy-kills-the-rooftop-sentinel, reified onto this parent hub.

candidates.quote: Kyle and Notch let fly together.
candidates.note: Kills a door guard; overlaps with sub-beat kyle-and-notch-kill-the-door-guards, reified onto this parent hub.

candidates.quote: Kyle and Notch let fly together.
candidates.note: Kills a door guard alongside Kyle; overlaps with sub-beat kyle-and-notch-kill-the-door-guards.

candidates.quote: Kyle was trying to nock another shaft to his string when a man in black mail flung a spear through his belly.
candidates.note: Kyle is killed mid-battle (distinct beat from his earlier AGENT_IN kill of the door guard).

candidates.quote: “Give the dying the gift of mercy and bind the others hand and foot for trial,” he commanded, and it was done.
candidates.note: Brotherhood without banners leader; commands the mercy-killing/binding after the fight and the subsequent trial that ends in Utt's hanging.

candidates.quote: Thoros and Lord Beric were everywhere, their swords swirling fire.
Arya saw Lem’s bright yellow cloak flapping behind him as he rode down the man who’d killed Kyle.
She spied Ned too, fighting at the lightning lord’s side.
candidates.note: ‘Ned’ = Edric Dayne, Beric's young squire (per node prose/wiki identification); the name alone in this single line doesn't disambiguate him from other Neds, hence tier-2.

candidates.quote: Lord Beric had left Beardless Dick and Mudge to guard them.
candidates.note: Guards Arya and Gendry away from the fighting; non-combat supportive role.

candidates.quote: Lord Beric had left Beardless Dick and Mudge to guard them.
candidates.note: Guards Arya and Gendry away from the fighting; non-combat supportive role.

candidates.quote: Jack-Be-Lucky, Harwin, and Merrit o’ Moontown braved the burning septry to search for captives.
candidates.note: Rescue of the captive brown brothers; non-combat.

candidates.quote: Jack-Be-Lucky, Harwin, and Merrit o’ Moontown braved the burning septry to search for captives.
candidates.note: Rescue of the captive brown brothers; non-combat.

candidates.quote: Jack-Be-Lucky, Harwin, and Merrit o’ Moontown braved the burning septry to search for captives.
candidates.note: Rescue of the captive brown brothers; non-combat.

candidates.quote: Septon Utt soon dangled beneath a tall elm, swinging slowly by the neck, as naked as his name day.
candidates.note: Hanged in the post-battle trial that this node's Aftermath section folds into its scope.

candidates.quote: Watty fell with a grunt, his bow slipping from his hand.
candidates.note: Struck down mid-battle.

candidates.quote: Arya watched from atop her horse, on the crest of the wooded ridge that overlooked the septry, mill, brewhouse, and stables and the desolation of weeds, burnt trees, and mud that surrounded them.
candidates.note: POV character directly observes the battle from the ridge (not shielded); text-anchor gate satisfied.

candidates.quote: Cousin, take this creature to the Wolf’s Den and cut off his head and hands.
candidates.note: Per prompt guidance, Wyman is the mastermind/agent of the whole staged deception even though the physical mechanics are delegated.

candidates.quote: Cousin, take this creature to the Wolf’s Den and cut off his head and hands.
candidates.note: Tasked executor of the staged custody/execution; takes Davos into Wolf's Den keeping. Overlaps with sub-beat wyman-publicly-orders-davos-execution, reified onto this parent hub.

candidates.quote: Ser Bartimus saw that the fingers of his left hand were shortened, the same as yours.
candidates.note: Mutilates the substitute's hand to sell the deception.

candidates.quote: Prince Aemond mounted Vhagar and went after him.
candidates.note: Pursues and fights Lucerys, resulting in the killing.

candidates.quote: Lucerys Velaryon died with his dragon, Munkun insists.
candidates.note: Killed in the dragon-fight/pursuit.

candidates.quote: Arrax fell, broken, to be swallowed by the storm-lashed waters of the bay.
candidates.note: Lucerys's dragon, killed alongside him.

candidates.quote: Vhagar was five times the size of her foe, the hardened survivor of a hundred battles.
candidates.note: Aemond's dragon; the one that physically overpowers Arrax.

candidates.quote: I saw the Mountain slay Raymun Darry with a single blow so terrible that it took Darry’s arm off at the elbow and killed the horse beneath him too.
candidates.note: Harwin's eyewitness account of the ambush.

candidates.quote: I saw the Mountain slay Raymun Darry with a single blow so terrible that it took Darry’s arm off at the elbow and killed the horse beneath him too.
candidates.note: Killed by Gregor Clegane.

candidates.quote: Gladden Wylde died there with him, and Lord Mallery was ridden down and drowned.
candidates.note: Killed in the ambush.

candidates.quote: Gladden Wylde died there with him, and Lord Mallery was ridden down and drowned.
candidates.note: Drowned fleeing the ambush.

candidates.quote: Alyn shouted commands and restored order to our ranks, and those still ahorse rallied around Thoros and cut our way free.
candidates.note: Rallies the broken king's-men line during the retreat.

candidates.quote: Alyn shouted commands and restored order to our ranks, and those still ahorse rallied around Thoros and cut our way free.
candidates.note: Survivors rally around him to cut free of the ambush.

candidates.quote: He sent Lord Beric instead, with twenty of his own men and twenty from Winterfell, me among them.
candidates.note: King's-men/Robert's-justice side; Eddard's chosen commander of the force ambushed at the Mummer's Ford.

candidates.quote: He sent Lord Beric instead, with twenty of his own men and twenty from Winterfell, me among them.
candidates.note: One of the twenty Winterfell guardsmen in Beric's ambushed force; narrates the battle to Arya as a survivor.

candidates.quote: Your Mountain stole my harvest and burned everything he could not carry off. He put my castle to the torch and raped one of my daughters.
candidates.note: Jonos Bracken to Jaime, describing the Mountain's men's raid.

candidates.quote: Your Mountain stole my harvest and burned everything he could not carry off. He put my castle to the torch and raped one of my daughters.
candidates.note: His castle, harvest, and daughter are the harm described.

candidates.quote: He smashed the Lords Vance and Piper at the Golden Tooth, and met the massed power of the Tullys under the walls of Riverrun.
candidates.note: Tywin recounting Jaime's victory to Tyrion.

candidates.quote: Ser Edmure Tully was taken captive, with many of his knights and bannermen.
candidates.note: Captured in the defeat.

candidates.quote: Lord Blackwood led a few survivors back to Riverrun, where Jaime has them under siege.
candidates.note: River-lords side; leads the surviving remnant back to Riverrun.

candidates.quote: Ser Gregor’s taken the castle.
candidates.note: Tywin reporting to Jaime that Gregor's men retook Harrenhal.

candidates.quote: The sellswords fled as soon as they knew we were coming, all but a few. One of the cooks opened a postern gate for us, to get back at Hoat for cutting off his foot.
candidates.note: Polliver's own account (‘we were coming’) confirms he was present for the retaking; Sandor's opening question (‘So Gregor took Harrenhal?’) shows Sandor was NOT present, so no Sandor edge is proposed here.

wiki_only.quote: Mathos sailed for Sisterton with a hundred warships. Although he never returned from war, his sons and descendants continued the fight against the northmen for a thousand years.
wiki_only.note: Vale/Arryn side; ancient TWOIAF-era history, no book-chapter text available in sources/chapters (no TWOIAF source cached, only novels/D&E/F&B).

wiki_only.quote: The Starks attacked Gulltown and, unable to overcome its walls, burned hundreds of ships in the city's harbor.
wiki_only.note: Northmen side; same TWOIAF-only sourcing caveat as R4-49.

no_node.quote: The man was a criminal, if that gives you any solace.
no_node.note: The unnamed tattooed criminal substituted and actually beheaded in Davos's place — the deception's true physical victim/patient. No node exists for him.

flags: war-across-the-water (event.war): entirely TWOIAF/wiki-sourced ancient history spanning ~1000 years; no matching book-chapter text exists in sources/chapters (only novels + D&E + F&B are cached, no TWOIAF). Only 2 wiki_only COMMANDS_IN rows proposed (Mathos II Arryn / House Stark) rather than exhaustively covering every named king across the centuries-long conflict.

wyman-manderly-stages-fake-execution-of-davos (event.deception): considered VICTIM_IN for Cersei/the small council (the deceived audience) but excluded per prompt guidance — they merely believe the lie and suffer no harm from it (Cersei gets what she wants and returns Wylis voluntarily), so they don't fit victim/patient; the true physical victim is the unnamed criminal, routed to no_node. Davos himself is protected by, not targeted by, this deception, so no role edge was proposed for him on this node.

burning-of-stone-hedge: wiki Origins attribute the order to Tywin Lannister, but no book-chapter quote directly shows Tywin ordering the Stone Hedge burning specifically (the general campaign order is grounded instead on battle-at-the-mummers-ford's own node, a separate event) — omitted a Tywin COMMANDS_IN edge here to avoid overreach; flagging in case another chunk's evidence closes this gap.

dany-lost-on-dothraki-sea (event.incident): honestly yields 0 candidates. Harm-gate blocks VICTIM_IN for Daenerys (incident is not a harm subtype); no other role in the vocabulary fits a solitary survival ordeal, and Drogon/Khal Jhaqo don't perform a definable role within this specific incident's scope (Drogon abandons her rather than acting on her behalf; Jhaqo's finding her is the incident's endpoint, not a role during it).

robb-receives-false-news-of-brans-death (event.incident): honestly yields 0 candidates. Harm-gate blocks VICTIM_IN for Robb Stark (incident, not a harm subtype); Greatjon Umber (the messenger who brings the false word) has no graph node, so no_node also doesn't apply — there's simply no name+role combination the current vocabulary and node inventory supports.

battle-at-the-burning-septry: proposed 16 role edges, above the '2-8 typical' guidance — justified because ASOS Arya VII names an unusually dense cast of individually load-bearing combatants/participants/victims for this single battle; trimmed out only the vaguer background mentions (e.g. unnamed Mummers, unnamed brown brothers).
